fg-alert.heading: You don’t qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit
fg-alert.p: Based on the information you provided, you can’t claim the earned income credit because your earned income is over the limit for your filing status and number of qualifying children.
For more information, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit.

section.p: Based on the information you provided, you are eligible for the Earned Income Tax Credit estimated amount of: *
This credit amount will reduce your taxes when you file your tax return with the IRS. If the credit reduces your tax to less than zero, you may get a refund.
section.h2: Next Steps: How to file for the EITC
section.p: In order to receive the estimated amount of *, you must file your federal tax return. If you claim the EITC, by law the IRS cannot issue your tax refund before mid-February , even the portion that is not associated with the EITC.
If you'd like to file your taxes online, you qualify to use the IRS Free File Program. Please note the program is only available from January through October.

p.a: File online now (opens in a new tab)

section.p: If you'd prefer to work with an in-person tax preparer, you have the option for doing this at an IRS Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) clinic.

p.a: Free in-person help (opens in a new tab)

section.p:
Since you have a qualifying child, you must fill out
Schedule EIC (PDF) (opens in a new tab)
and attach it to your
Form 1040 (PDF) (opens in a new tab)
or
Form 1040-SR (PDF) (opens in a new tab)
for tax year .
The table below is a summary of the information you entered in the tool. If any of this information is incorrect please click edit to update it, otherwise, it could affect your results.

You can use the
IRS2Go app (opens in a new tab)
to check your refund status, make a payment, find free tax preparation assistance, sign up for helpful tax tips, and more!
